message:
test+build(lexical): cover 32-thread CASS cap in cass_writer_config tests; pull UserOperation import inline; drop dotenvy in favour of std::env

Three small follow-ups to the prior CASS_MAX_WRITER_THREADS bump.

Tests
-----
`cass_writer_config_for_parallelism` had two assertion blocks that
encoded the old 8-thread cap:
  - 8-core machine: 8 threads × 128 MB = 1 GB
  - 64-core machine: clamped to 8 threads (1 GB)

Both are now updated to the new 32-thread cap and a third 32-core
case is added so the test trio covers under-cap (8), at-cap (32),
and over-cap (64) behaviour explicitly:
  - 8-core:  8 threads, ≈ 1 GB heap
  - 32-core: 32 threads, ≈ 4 GB heap (at the new cap)
  - 64-core: still 32 threads, ≈ 4 GB heap (clamped)

Build hygiene
-------------
- `tantivy::indexer::UserOperation` now imported on its own line
  instead of being mixed into the main `use tantivy::{...}` block.
  The main block is for top-level tantivy re-exports; UserOperation
  lives under the `indexer` submodule and reading the imports as a
  flat list previously implied (incorrectly) that
  `tantivy::UserOperation` was a top-level re-export.

- Switched `dotenvy::var("CASS_TANTIVY_MAX_WRITER_THREADS")` →
  `std::env::var(...)`.  frankensearch-lexical is a library used
  by frankensearch consumers; it has no business loading the
  caller's `.env` file.  The caller (e.g. cass) is the one that
  should own dotenvy initialization and then export the variable
  into the process environment that std::env reads.  Removes the
  unnecessary dotenvy dependency lookup from this crate.

- Pure cargo-fmt reflow of the
  `d.add_text(fields.preview, cass_build_preview(...))` call back
  onto a single line; the multi-line form was a copy-paste artefact
  that prettier expanded inadvertently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
